  export default defineNuxtConfig({

#title
@witchcraft/ui 🪄

#description
Opinionated, batteries-included, [Vue](https://vuejs.org){.text-highlighted.hover:underline rel=""nofollow""}/[Nuxt](https://nuxt.com/){.text-highlighted.hover:underline rel=""nofollow""} component library built on [Reka UI](https://reka-ui.com/){.text-highlighted.hover:underline rel=""nofollow""} with [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/){.text-highlighted.hover:underline rel=""nofollow""}. Accessibility-first, themable via [metamorphosis](https://github.com/alanscodelog/metamorphosis){.text-highlighted.hover:underline rel=""nofollow""}.

#title
Features

#features
  :::u-page-feature
  ---
  icon: i-lucide-shield-check
  ---
  #title
  Accessibility First

  #description
  Proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support built in from the ground up.
  :::

  :::u-page-feature
  ---
  icon: i-lucide-palette
  ---
  #title
  Themable via Metamorphosis

  #description
  Metamorphosis provides a way to customize the theme at runtime.
  :::

  :::u-page-feature
  ---
  icon: i-lucide-minimize
  ---
  #title
  Unstyle Option

  #description
  Simple components have an unstyle option for minimal styling when you need it.
  :::
::


# Introduction

@witchcraft/ui is an opinionated, batteries-included [Vue](https://vuejs.org){rel="&#x22;nofollow&#x22;"}/[Nuxt](https://nuxt.com/){rel="&#x22;nofollow&#x22;"} component library built on [Reka UI](https://reka-ui.com/){rel="&#x22;nofollow&#x22;"} with [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/){rel="&#x22;nofollow&#x22;"}. Accessibility-first, themable via [metamorphosis](https://github.com/alanscodelog/metamorphosis){rel="&#x22;nofollow&#x22;"}.

While the docs have storybook embeds, all full interactive examples and demos are available in [Storybook](https://witchcraftjs.github.io/ui/storybook){rel="&#x22;nofollow&#x22;"}.


# Installation (Nuxt)

## Install

```bash
npm install @witchcraft/ui
```

## Nuxt Setup

Add the module to your `nuxt.config.ts`:

```ts [nuxt.config.ts]
export default defineNuxtConfig({
    modules: ['@witchcraft/ui']
})
```

The module automatically sets up:

- Tailwind CSS v4 with a custom `witchcraft-ui.css` file
- `unplugin-icons/nuxt` for icon support
- Reka UI via `reka-ui/nuxt`

## Tailwind CSS

Import the generated stylesheet in your Tailwind CSS file:

```css [~/assets/css/tailwind.css]
@import "@tailwindcss" source("../../app");
@import "../../.nuxt/witchcraft-ui.css";
```

## Icons

The module installs `unplugin-icons/nuxt` and configures it with custom styles. You can disable this with the `includeUnpluginIconsPlugins` option.

## Helpers

Some helpers are not auto-imported. Import them from `#witchcraft-ui-helpers`:

```ts
import { someHelper } from "#witchcraft-ui-helpers"
```

Anything else can be imported from `#witchcraft-ui`.


# Installation (Standalone)

## Vite Setup

```ts
import { defineConfig } from "vite"

import { WitchcraftUiResolver } from "@witchcraft/ui/build/WitchcraftUiResolver"
import IconsResolver from "unplugin-icons/resolver"
import Icons from "unplugin-icons/vite"
import Components from "unplugin-vue-components/vite"
import { unpluginIconViteOptions } from "@witchcraft/ui/build/unpluginIconViteOptions"

export default defineConfig({
    plugins: [
        vue(),
        Components({
            resolvers: [
                IconsResolver(),
                WitchcraftUiResolver({
                    prefix: "W",
                    filter: (name) => name !== "..." // optionally filter components
                })
            ],
            dts: "./types/components.d.ts"
        }),
        Icons(unpluginIconViteOptions)
    ]
})
```

See [@witchcraft/editor/vite.config.dev.ts](https://github.com/witchcraftjs/ui){rel="&#x22;nofollow&#x22;"} for a complete example.

## Tailwind CSS

Import the required styles:

```css
@import "@witchcraft/ui/utils.css";
@import "@witchcraft/ui/base.css";
@import "@witchcraft/ui/animations.css";
@source "/path/to/node_modules/@witchcraft/ui/src/runtime/components";
```

Base contains animation keyframes, basic styles for Vue animations, and some basic global styles.

## tailwind-merge

The library provides a customized `twMerge` instance at `@witchcraft/ui/utils/twMerge.js`. You will need to use it or import the options it extends and customize your own `twMerge` instance.

## Icons

This library uses `unplugin-icons` to provide icons. It installs `@iconify/json` as a dependency.

## Types

While components should be correctly typed, the library internally extends Vue's interfaces to allow `data-*` attributes and some missing ARIA attributes (like `aria-description`).

You may need to add the following in a global `.d.ts`:

```ts
import { GlobalComponentTypes } from "@witchcraft/ui"

declare module "@vue/runtime-core" {
    interface GlobalComponents extends GlobalComponentTypes {}
}

declare module "vue" {
    interface HTMLAttributes {
        // ...
    }
    interface AriaAttributes {
        // ...
    }
    interface ComponentCustomProps {
        // ...
    }
}

export {}
```

Note that using the `strictTemplates` compiler option can cause weird issues with fallthrough props.


# Usage

## Components

Components are auto-imported when using the Nuxt module. For standalone usage, they are auto-imported via `unplugin-vue-components` with the `WitchcraftUiResolver`.

```vue
<template>
    <WButton>Click me</WButton>
</template>
```

## Props

Most components accept standard HTML attributes via fallthrough props which are sent to the element that most "embodies" the component, e.g. $attrs goes to the checkbox root not it's wrapper (this is documented per component). Some components also accept typed props for specific parts or to override reka's props (e.g. root-props):

```vue
<WCheckbox wrapper-attrs="{ class: 'my-wrapper-class' }" />
```

## Icons

The module installs `unplugin-icons/nuxt` and configures it with custom styles. Use icons with the `WIcon` component:

```vue
<WIcon><i-lucide-check/></WIcon>
```

`WIcon` is a simple wrapper that applies `inline-block` display to the icon in its slot.

## Helpers

Some helpers are not auto-imported. Import them from `#witchcraft-ui-helpers`:

```ts
import { someHelper } from "#witchcraft-ui-helpers"
```

Anything else can be imported from `#witchcraft-ui`.


# WAsyncValidatedInput

This is for usage with @witchcraft/nuxt-utils's useAsyncValidation for handling
complex validation scenarios (client+server) as in the case of a username.

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WAuth

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WAuthLocalUser

See [WAuth](https://witchcraftjs.github.io/ui/components/w-auth) for storybook stories.

:component-docs


# WButton

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WCheckbox

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WColorInput

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WColorPicker

:storybook-embed

::component-docs
| Variable           | Description                               |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| `--slider-size`    | Size of the slider                        |
| `--contrast-dark`  | Dark contrast value                       |
| `--contrast-light` | Light contrast value                      |
| `--fg`             | Overrides Reka UI slider foreground color |
| `--bg`             | Overrides Reka UI slider background color |
::

:storybook-embed

:component-docs

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WDatePicker

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WFileInput

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WIcon

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WMetamorphosisTheme

This is a slightly experimental component. Not all types of themes are supported, but the
most common will work. There is no save/load mechanism yet, though you can hook
into metamorphosis's internal events to get notified of theme changes.

:storybook-embed

::component-docs
| Variable        | Description                               |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `--slider-size` | Size of the slider handle and color strip |
| `--chart-size`  | Size of the numerical chart               |
::

:component-docs

:storybook-embed

:component-docs


# WNotifications

:storybook-embed

::component-docs
| Variable               | Description                    |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `--notification-width` | Width of notification elements |
